description: Cluster is the Schema for the Clusters API. A Google Cloud Redis Cluster instance.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: ClusterSpec defines the desired state of Cluster

allowFewerZonesDeployment.description:
Allows customers to specify if they are okay with deploying a multi-zone
cluster in less than 3 zones. Once set, if there is a zonal outage during
the cluster creation, the cluster will only be deployed in 2 zones, and
stay within the 2 zones for its lifecycle.

authorizationMode.description:
Optional. The authorization mode of the Redis cluster. If not provided, auth feature is disabled for the cluster.
Default value is AUTH_MODE_DISABLED.
Possible values are: AUTH_MODE_UNSPECIFIED, AUTH_MODE_IAM_AUTH, AUTH_MODE_DISABLED.

automatedBackupConfig.description:
The automated backup config for a instance.
Structure is documented below.

fixedFrequencySchedule.description:
Trigger automated backups at a fixed frequency.
Structure is documented below.

startTime.description:
Required. Start time of the window in UTC time.
Structure is documented below.

hours.description:
Hours of day in 24 hour format. Should be from 0 to 23.
An API may choose to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

retention.description:
How long to keep automated backups before the backups are deleted.
The value should be between 1 day and 365 days. If not specified, the default value is 35 days.
A duration in seconds with up to nine fractional digits, ending with 's'. Example: "3.5s".

crossClusterReplicationConfig.description: field to the configuration file to match the latest value in the state.

cluster.description: The full resource path of the secondary cluster in the format: projects/{project}/locations/{region}/clusters/{cluster-id}

clusterRef.description: Reference to a Cluster in redis to populate cluster.

resolution.description:
Resolution specifies whether resolution of this reference is required.
The default is 'Required', which means the reconcile will fail if the
reference cannot be resolved. 'Optional' means this reference will be
a no-op if it cannot be resolved.

resolve.description:
Resolve specifies when this reference should be resolved. The default
is 'IfNotPresent', which will attempt to resolve the reference only when
the corresponding field is not present. Use 'Always' to resolve the
reference on every reconcile.

clusterSelector.description: Selector for a Cluster in redis to populate cluster.

matchControllerRef.description:
MatchControllerRef ensures an object with the same controller reference
as the selecting object is selected.

matchLabels.description: MatchLabels ensures an object with matching labels is selected.

resolution.description:
Resolution specifies whether resolution of this reference is required.
The default is 'Required', which means the reconcile will fail if the
reference cannot be resolved. 'Optional' means this reference will be
a no-op if it cannot be resolved.

resolve.description:
Resolve specifies when this reference should be resolved. The default
is 'IfNotPresent', which will attempt to resolve the reference only when
the corresponding field is not present. Use 'Always' to resolve the
reference on every reconcile.

secondaryClusters.description: list with the new secondaries. The new secondaries are the current primary and other secondary clusters(if any).

cluster.description: The full resource path of the secondary cluster in the format: projects/{project}/locations/{region}/clusters/{cluster-id}

deletionProtectionEnabled.description:
Optional. Indicates if the cluster is deletion protected or not.
If the value if set to true, any delete cluster operation will fail.
Default value is true.

gcsSource.description:
Backups stored in Cloud Storage buckets. The Cloud Storage buckets need to be the same region as the clusters.
Structure is documented below.

uris.description: URIs of the GCS objects to import. Example: gs://bucket1/object1, gs://bucket2/folder2/object2

kmsKey.description: The KMS key used to encrypt the at-rest data of the cluster.

maintenancePolicy.description:
Maintenance policy for a cluster
Structure is documented below.

weeklyMaintenanceWindow.description:
Optional. Maintenance window that is applied to resources covered by this policy.
Minimum 1. For the current version, the maximum number
of weekly_window is expected to be one.
Structure is documented below.

day.description: Required. The day of week that maintenance updates occur.

startTime.description:
Required. Start time of the window in UTC time.
Structure is documented below.

hours.description:
Hours of day in 24 hour format. Should be from 0 to 23.
An API may choose to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

minutes.description: Minutes of hour of day. Must be from 0 to 59.

nanos.description: Fractions of seconds in nanoseconds. Must be from 0 to 999,999,999.

seconds.description:
Seconds of minutes of the time. Must normally be from 0 to 59.
An API may allow the value 60 if it allows leap-seconds.

managedBackupSource.description:
Backups that generated and managed by memorystore.
Structure is documented below.

nodeType.description:
The nodeType for the Redis cluster.
If not provided, REDIS_HIGHMEM_MEDIUM will be used as default
Possible values are: REDIS_SHARED_CORE_NANO, REDIS_HIGHMEM_MEDIUM, REDIS_HIGHMEM_XLARGE, REDIS_STANDARD_SMALL.

persistenceConfig.description:
Persistence config (RDB, AOF) for the cluster.
Structure is documented below.

aofConfig.description:
AOF configuration. This field will be ignored if mode is not AOF.
Structure is documented below.

mode.description: Optional. Controls whether Persistence features are enabled. If not provided, the existing value will be used.

rdbConfig.description:
RDB configuration. This field will be ignored if mode is not RDB.
Structure is documented below.

rdbSnapshotPeriod.description: Optional. Available snapshot periods for scheduling.

rdbSnapshotStartTime.description:
The time that the first snapshot was/will be attempted, and to which
future snapshots will be aligned.
If not provided, the current time will be used.

project.description:
The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

pscConfigs.description:
Required. Each PscConfig configures the consumer network where two
network addresses will be designated to the cluster for client access.
Currently, only one PscConfig is supported.
Structure is documented below.

network.description:
Required. The consumer network where the network address of
the discovery endpoint will be reserved, in the form of
projects/{network_project_id_or_number}/global/networks/{network_id}.

networkRef.description: Reference to a Network in compute to populate network.

resolution.description:
Resolution specifies whether resolution of this reference is required.
The default is 'Required', which means the reconcile will fail if the
reference cannot be resolved. 'Optional' means this reference will be
a no-op if it cannot be resolved.

resolve.description:
Resolve specifies when this reference should be resolved. The default
is 'IfNotPresent', which will attempt to resolve the reference only when
the corresponding field is not present. Use 'Always' to resolve the
reference on every reconcile.

networkSelector.description: Selector for a Network in compute to populate network.

matchControllerRef.description:
MatchControllerRef ensures an object with the same controller reference
as the selecting object is selected.

matchLabels.description: MatchLabels ensures an object with matching labels is selected.

resolution.description:
Resolution specifies whether resolution of this reference is required.
The default is 'Required', which means the reconcile will fail if the
reference cannot be resolved. 'Optional' means this reference will be
a no-op if it cannot be resolved.

resolve.description:
Resolve specifies when this reference should be resolved. The default
is 'IfNotPresent', which will attempt to resolve the reference only when
the corresponding field is not present. Use 'Always' to resolve the
reference on every reconcile.

redisConfigs.description:
Configure Redis Cluster behavior using a subset of native Redis configuration parameters.
Please check Memorystore documentation for the list of supported parameters:
https://cloud.google.com/memorystore/docs/cluster/supported-instance-configurations

region.description: The name of the region of the Redis cluster.

replicaCount.description: Optional. The number of replica nodes per shard.

shardCount.description: Required. Number of shards for the Redis cluster.

transitEncryptionMode.description:
Optional. The in-transit encryption for the Redis cluster.
If not provided, encryption is disabled for the cluster.
Default value is TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_DISABLED.
Possible values are: TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_UNSPECIFIED, TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_DISABLED, TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_SERVER_AUTHENTICATION.

zoneDistributionConfig.description:
Immutable. Zone distribution config for Memorystore Redis cluster.
Structure is documented below.

mode.description:
Immutable. The mode for zone distribution for Memorystore Redis cluster.
If not provided, MULTI_ZONE will be used as default
Possible values are: MULTI_ZONE, SINGLE_ZONE.

zone.description: Immutable. The zone for single zone Memorystore Redis cluster.

initProvider.description:
THIS IS A BETA FIELD. It will be honored
unless the Management Policies feature flag is disabled.
InitProvider holds the same fields as ForProvider, with the exception
of Identifier and other resource reference fields. The fields that are
in InitProvider are merged into ForProvider when the resource is created.
The same fields are also added to the terraform ignore_changes hook, to
avoid updating them after creation. This is useful for fields that are
required on creation, but we do not desire to update them after creation,
for example because of an external controller is managing them, like an
autoscaler.

stay within the 2 zones for its lifecycle.

Default value is AUTH_MODE_DISABLED.
Possible values are: AUTH_MODE_UNSPECIFIED, AUTH_MODE_IAM_AUTH, AUTH_MODE_DISABLED.

managementPolicies.description:
THIS IS A BETA FIELD. It is on by default but can be opted out
through a Crossplane feature flag.
ManagementPolicies specify the array of actions Crossplane is allowed to
take on the managed and external resources.
See the design doc for more information: https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane/blob/499895a25d1a1a0ba1604944ef98ac7a1a71f197/design/design-doc-observe-only-resources.md?plain=1#L223
and this one: https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane/blob/444267e84783136daa93568b364a5f01228cacbe/design/one-pager-ignore-changes.md

items.description:
A ManagementAction represents an action that the Crossplane controllers
can take on an external resource.

providerConfigRef.description:
ProviderConfigReference specifies how the provider that will be used to
create, observe, update, and delete this managed resource should be
configured.

writeConnectionSecretToRef.description:
WriteConnectionSecretToReference specifies the namespace and name of a
Secret to which any connection details for this managed resource should
be written. Connection details frequently include the endpoint, username,
and password required to connect to the managed resource.

x-kubernetes-validations.rule: !('*' in self.managementPolicies || 'Create' in self.managementPolicies || 'Update' in self.managementPolicies) || has(self.forProvider.shardCount) || (has(self.initProvider) && has(self.initProvider.shardCount))

status.description: ClusterStatus defines the observed state of Cluster.

allowFewerZonesDeployment.description:
Allows customers to specify if they are okay with deploying a multi-zone
cluster in less than 3 zones. Once set, if there is a zonal outage during
the cluster creation, the cluster will only be deployed in 2 zones, and
stay within the 2 zones for its lifecycle.

authorizationMode.description:
Optional. The authorization mode of the Redis cluster. If not provided, auth feature is disabled for the cluster.
Default value is AUTH_MODE_DISABLED.
Possible values are: AUTH_MODE_UNSPECIFIED, AUTH_MODE_IAM_AUTH, AUTH_MODE_DISABLED.

automatedBackupConfig.description:
The automated backup config for a instance.
Structure is documented below.

fixedFrequencySchedule.description:
Trigger automated backups at a fixed frequency.
Structure is documented below.

startTime.description:
Required. Start time of the window in UTC time.
Structure is documented below.

hours.description:
Hours of day in 24 hour format. Should be from 0 to 23.
An API may choose to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

retention.description:
How long to keep automated backups before the backups are deleted.
The value should be between 1 day and 365 days. If not specified, the default value is 35 days.
A duration in seconds with up to nine fractional digits, ending with 's'. Example: "3.5s".

backupCollection.description:
The backup collection full resource name.
Example: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/backupCollections/{collection}

createTime.description:
The timestamp associated with the cluster creation request. A timestamp in
RFC3339 UTC "Zulu" format, with nanosecond resolution and up to nine fractional
digits. Examples: "2014-10-02T15:01:23Z" and "2014-10-02T15:01:23.045123456Z".

crossClusterReplicationConfig.description: field to the configuration file to match the latest value in the state.

membership.description:
(Output)
An output only view of all the member clusters participating in cross cluster replication. This field is populated for all the member clusters irrespective of their cluster role.
Structure is documented below.

primaryCluster.description:
(Output)
Details of the primary cluster that is used as the replication source for all the secondary clusters.
Structure is documented below.

cluster.description: The full resource path of the secondary cluster in the format: projects/{project}/locations/{region}/clusters/{cluster-id}

uid.description: System assigned, unique identifier for the cluster.

secondaryClusters.description:
(Output)
List of secondary clusters that are replicating from the primary cluster.
Structure is documented below.

cluster.description: The full resource path of the secondary cluster in the format: projects/{project}/locations/{region}/clusters/{cluster-id}

uid.description: System assigned, unique identifier for the cluster.

cluster.description: The full resource path of the secondary cluster in the format: projects/{project}/locations/{region}/clusters/{cluster-id}

uid.description: System assigned, unique identifier for the cluster.

secondaryClusters.description: list with the new secondaries. The new secondaries are the current primary and other secondary clusters(if any).

cluster.description: The full resource path of the secondary cluster in the format: projects/{project}/locations/{region}/clusters/{cluster-id}

uid.description: System assigned, unique identifier for the cluster.

updateTime.description:
(Output)
The last time cross cluster replication config was updated.

deletionProtectionEnabled.description:
Optional. Indicates if the cluster is deletion protected or not.
If the value if set to true, any delete cluster operation will fail.
Default value is true.

discoveryEndpoints.description:
Output only. Endpoints created on each given network,
for Redis clients to connect to the cluster.
Currently only one endpoint is supported.
Structure is documented below.

address.description: Output only. Network address of the exposed Redis endpoint used by clients to connect to the service.

port.description: Output only. The port number of the exposed Redis endpoint.

pscConfig.description:
Output only. Customer configuration for where the endpoint
is created and accessed from.
Structure is documented below.

network.description: The consumer network where the IP address resides, in the form of projects/{projectId}/global/networks/{network_id}.

gcsSource.description:
Backups stored in Cloud Storage buckets. The Cloud Storage buckets need to be the same region as the clusters.
Structure is documented below.

uris.description: URIs of the GCS objects to import. Example: gs://bucket1/object1, gs://bucket2/folder2/object2

id.description: an identifier for the resource with format projects/{{project}}/locations/{{region}}/clusters/{{name}}

kmsKey.description: The KMS key used to encrypt the at-rest data of the cluster.

maintenancePolicy.description:
Maintenance policy for a cluster
Structure is documented below.

createTime.description:
(Output)
Output only. The time when the policy was created.
A timestamp in RFC3339 UTC "Zulu" format, with nanosecond
resolution and up to nine fractional digits.

updateTime.description:
(Output)
Output only. The time when the policy was last updated.
A timestamp in RFC3339 UTC "Zulu" format, with nanosecond
resolution and up to nine fractional digits.

weeklyMaintenanceWindow.description:
Optional. Maintenance window that is applied to resources covered by this policy.
Minimum 1. For the current version, the maximum number
of weekly_window is expected to be one.
Structure is documented below.

day.description: Required. The day of week that maintenance updates occur.

duration.description:
(Output)
Output only. Duration of the maintenance window.
The current window is fixed at 1 hour.
A duration in seconds with up to nine fractional digits,
terminated by 's'. Example: "3.5s".

startTime.description:
Required. Start time of the window in UTC time.
Structure is documented below.

hours.description:
Hours of day in 24 hour format. Should be from 0 to 23.
An API may choose to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

minutes.description: Minutes of hour of day. Must be from 0 to 59.

nanos.description: Fractions of seconds in nanoseconds. Must be from 0 to 999,999,999.

seconds.description:
Seconds of minutes of the time. Must normally be from 0 to 59.
An API may allow the value 60 if it allows leap-seconds.

maintenanceSchedule.description:
Upcoming maintenance schedule.
Structure is documented below.

endTime.description:
(Output)
Output only. The end time of any upcoming scheduled maintenance for this cluster.
A timestamp in RFC3339 UTC "Zulu" format, with nanosecond
resolution and up to nine fractional digits.

scheduleDeadlineTime.description:
(Output)
Output only. The deadline that the maintenance schedule start time
can not go beyond, including reschedule.
A timestamp in RFC3339 UTC "Zulu" format, with nanosecond
resolution and up to nine fractional digits.

startTime.description:
(Output)
Output only. The start time of any upcoming scheduled maintenance for this cluster.
A timestamp in RFC3339 UTC "Zulu" format, with nanosecond
resolution and up to nine fractional digits.

managedBackupSource.description:
Backups that generated and managed by memorystore.
Structure is documented below.

managedServerCa.description:
Cluster's Certificate Authority. This field will only be populated if Redis Cluster's transit_encryption_mode is TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_SERVER_AUTHENTICATION
Structure is documented below.

caCerts.description:
(Output)
The PEM encoded CA certificate chains for redis managed server authentication
Structure is documented below.

certificates.description:
(Output)
The certificates that form the CA chain, from leaf to root order

nodeType.description:
The nodeType for the Redis cluster.
If not provided, REDIS_HIGHMEM_MEDIUM will be used as default
Possible values are: REDIS_SHARED_CORE_NANO, REDIS_HIGHMEM_MEDIUM, REDIS_HIGHMEM_XLARGE, REDIS_STANDARD_SMALL.

RDB configuration. This field will be ignored if mode is not RDB.

rdbSnapshotPeriod.description: Optional. Available snapshot periods for scheduling.

rdbSnapshotStartTime.description:
The time that the first snapshot was/will be attempted, and to which
future snapshots will be aligned.
If not provided, the current time will be used.

preciseSizeGb.description: Output only. Redis memory precise size in GB for the entire cluster.

project.description:
The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

pscConfigs.description:
Required. Each PscConfig configures the consumer network where two
network addresses will be designated to the cluster for client access.
Currently, only one PscConfig is supported.
Structure is documented below.

network.description:
Required. The consumer network where the network address of
the discovery endpoint will be reserved, in the form of
projects/{network_project_id_or_number}/global/networks/{network_id}.

pscConnections.description:
Output only. PSC connections for discovery of the cluster topology and accessing the cluster.
Structure is documented below.

address.description: Output only. The IP allocated on the consumer network for the PSC forwarding rule.

forwardingRule.description: Output only. The URI of the consumer side forwarding rule. Example: projects/{projectNumOrId}/regions/us-east1/forwardingRules/{resourceId}.

network.description: The consumer network where the IP address resides, in the form of projects/{projectId}/global/networks/{network_id}.

projectId.description: Output only. The consumer projectId where the forwarding rule is created from.

pscConnectionId.description: Output only. The PSC connection id of the forwarding rule connected to the service attachment.

pscServiceAttachments.description:
Service attachment details to configure Psc connections.
Structure is documented below.

connectionType.description:
(Output)
Type of a PSC connection targeting this service attachment.

serviceAttachment.description:
(Output)
Service attachment URI which your self-created PscConnection should use as

redisConfigs.description:
Configure Redis Cluster behavior using a subset of native Redis configuration parameters.
Please check Memorystore documentation for the list of supported parameters:
https://cloud.google.com/memorystore/docs/cluster/supported-instance-configurations

region.description: The name of the region of the Redis cluster.

replicaCount.description: Optional. The number of replica nodes per shard.

shardCount.description: Required. Number of shards for the Redis cluster.

sizeGb.description: Output only. Redis memory size in GB for the entire cluster.

state.description: The current state of this cluster. Can be CREATING, READY, UPDATING, DELETING and SUSPENDED

stateInfo.description:
Output only. Additional information about the current state of the cluster.
Structure is documented below.

updateInfo.description:
A nested object resource.
Structure is documented below.

targetReplicaCount.description: Target number of replica nodes per shard.

targetShardCount.description: Target number of shards for redis cluster.

transitEncryptionMode.description:
Optional. The in-transit encryption for the Redis cluster.
If not provided, encryption is disabled for the cluster.
Default value is TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_DISABLED.
Possible values are: TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_UNSPECIFIED, TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_DISABLED, TRANSIT_ENCRYPTION_MODE_SERVER_AUTHENTICATION.

uid.description: System assigned, unique identifier for the cluster.

zoneDistributionConfig.description:
Immutable. Zone distribution config for Memorystore Redis cluster.
Structure is documented below.

mode.description:
Immutable. The mode for zone distribution for Memorystore Redis cluster.
If not provided, MULTI_ZONE will be used as default
Possible values are: MULTI_ZONE, SINGLE_ZONE.

zone.description: Immutable. The zone for single zone Memorystore Redis cluster.

items.description: A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime.description:
LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one
status to another.

message.description:
A Message containing details about this condition's last transition from
one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration.description:
ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon.
For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions[x].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date
with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason.description: A Reason for this condition's last transition from one status to another.

status.description: Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type.description:
Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to
a resource at any point in time.

observedGeneration.description:
ObservedGeneration is the latest metadata.generation
which resulted in either a ready state, or stalled due to error
it can not recover from without human intervention.
